_comment: Default DataMorgana-style config. Mirrors the categorizations used in the CIKM RAG Study paper. Edit freely — add/remove categorizations and categories as you wish. Probabilities within each categorization must sum to 1.0.

categories.name: factoid
categories.description: A question seeking a specific, concise piece of information or a short fact about a particular subject, such as a name, date, or number.

categories.name: open_ended
categories.description: A question inviting detailed or exploratory responses, encouraging discussion or elaboration.

categories.name: direct
categories.description: A question that does not contain any premise or any information about the user.

categories.name: with_premise
categories.description: A question starting with a very short premise, where the user reveals their needs or some information about themselves.

categories.name: concise_and_natural
categories.description: A question phrased in the way people typically speak, reflecting everyday language use, without formal or artificial structure. It is a concise direct question consisting of less than 10 words.

categories.name: verbose_and_natural
categories.description: A question phrased in the way people typically speak, reflecting everyday language use, without formal or artificial structure. It is a relatively long question consisting of more than 9 words.

categories.name: short_search_query
categories.description: A question phrased as a typed web query for search engines (only keywords, without punctuation and without a natural-sounding structure). It consists of less than 7 words.

categories.name: long_search_query
categories.description: A question phrased as a typed web query for search engines (only keywords, without punctuation and without a natural-sounding structure). It consists of more than 6 words.

categories.name: similar_to_document
categories.description: A question phrased using the same terminology and phrases appearing in the document.

categories.name: distant_from_document
categories.description: A question phrased using terms completely different from the ones appearing in the document.

categories.name: expert
categories.description: A specialized user with deep understanding of the corpus.

categories.name: novice
categories.description: A regular user with no understanding of specialized terms.
